# CLI Reference

Brain Dump CLI provides full resource management, database utilities, and developer workflow commands.

All commands output JSON by default. Add `--pretty` for human-readable output.

## Quick Reference

### Resources

| Resource     | Description                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `project`    | List, find, create, delete projects â€” _list, find, create, delete_                                                                                                       |
| `ticket`     | Create, list, get, update, delete tickets â€” _create, list, get, update, update-status, update-criterion, update-attachment, list-by-epic, link-files, get-files, delete_ |
| `epic`       | Create, list, update, delete epics â€” _create, list, update, delete, reconcile-learnings, get-learnings_                                                                  |
| `workflow`   | Start work, complete work, start epic, launch Ralph â€” _start-work, complete-work, start-epic, launch-ticket, launch-epic_                                                |
| `comment`    | Add and list ticket comments â€” _add, list_                                                                                                                               |
| `review`     | Submit findings, generate demos, manage reviews â€” _submit-finding, mark-fixed, check-complete, generate-demo, get-demo, submit-feedback, update-demo-step, get-findings_ |
| `session`    | Create, update, complete Ralph sessions â€” _create, update, complete, get, list, update-state, emit-event, get-events, clear-events_                                      |
| `git`        | Link commits, PRs, sync ticket links â€” _link-commit, link-pr, sync_                                                                                                      |
| `telemetry`  | Start, end, get, list telemetry sessions, record token usage â€” _start, end, get, list, log-tool, log-prompt, log-context, record-usage, recalculate-costs_               |
| `files`      | Link files to tickets, find tickets by file â€” _link, get-tickets_                                                                                                        |
| `tasks`      | Save, get, clear Claude task lists â€” _save, get, clear, snapshots_                                                                                                       |
| `compliance` | Conversation logging for compliance auditing â€” _start, log, end, list, export, archive_                                                                                  |
| `settings`   | Get and update project settings â€” _get, update_                                                                                                                          |
| `transfer`   | Export and import .braindump archives â€” _export-epic, export-project, import, preview_                                                                                   |
| `admin`      | Backup, restore, check, doctor, health â€” _backup, restore, check, doctor, health_                                                                                        |

### Top-Level Commands

| Command                  | Description                                                     |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `brain-dump completions` | Generate shell completion scripts                               |
| `brain-dump open`        | Open Brain Dump UI in the browser                               |
| `brain-dump status`      | Project dashboard: ticket summary, active work, recent activity |
| `brain-dump init`        | Register current directory as a Brain Dump project              |
| `brain-dump search`      | Full-text ticket search with FTS5 and LIKE fallback             |
| `brain-dump context`     | Full ticket context: details, comments, findings, and criteria  |
| `brain-dump log`         | Chronological activity stream across tickets                    |

### Backward-Compatible Shortcuts

| Shortcut                          | Equivalent                                    |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `brain-dump backup`               | `brain-dump admin backup`                     |
| `brain-dump restore`              | `brain-dump admin restore`                    |
| `brain-dump check`                | `brain-dump admin check`                      |
| `brain-dump doctor`               | `brain-dump admin doctor`                     |
| `brain-dump export --epic <id>`   | `brain-dump transfer export-epic --epic <id>` |
| `brain-dump import --file <path>` | `brain-dump transfer import --file <path>`    |

---

## Top-Level Commands

### brain-dump completions

Generate shell completion scripts

```bash
brain-dump completions
```

_No flags._

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump completions zsh
brain-dump completions bash
brain-dump completions fish
```

### brain-dump open

Open Brain Dump UI in the browser

```bash
brain-dump open [--port <n>]
```

| Flag     | Type   | Required | Description                 |
| -------- | ------ | -------- | --------------------------- |
| `--port` | number | No       | Port number (default: 4242) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump open
brain-dump open --port 3000
```

### brain-dump status

Project dashboard: ticket summary, active work, recent activity

```bash
brain-dump status [--project <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                             |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID (or auto-detect from cwd)    |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max recent activity items (default: 10) |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)   |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump status --pretty
brain-dump status --project abc --limit 5 --pretty
```

### brain-dump init

Register current directory as a Brain Dump project

```bash
brain-dump init [--name <value>] [--color <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                                                     |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--name`   | string  | No       | Project name (default: package.json name or directory basename) |
| `--color`  | string  | No       | Color hex (e.g. #3b82f6)                                        |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                           |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump init
brain-dump init --name "My App" --color "#3b82f6"
brain-dump init --pretty
```

### brain-dump search

Full-text ticket search with FTS5 and LIKE fallback

```bash
brain-dump search [--project <value>] [--status <backlog|ready|in_progress|ai_review|human_review|done>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                                                          |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                                                                           |
| `--status`  | enum    | No       | Filter by ticket status (backlog, ready, in_progress, ai_review, human_review, done) |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                                                                          |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                                |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump search "workflow" --pretty
brain-dump search "bug" --project abc --status in_progress --pretty
```

### brain-dump context

Full ticket context: details, comments, findings, and criteria

```bash
brain-dump context --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump context --ticket abc123 --pretty
brain-dump context --ticket abc123
```

### brain-dump log

Chronological activity stream across tickets

```bash
brain-dump log [--project <value>] [--ticket <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID (or auto-detect from cwd)  |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max entries (default: 20)             |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump log --pretty
brain-dump log --project abc --limit 10 --pretty
brain-dump log --ticket abc --pretty
```

---

## project

List, find, create, delete projects

### brain-dump project list

List all registered projects

```bash
brain-dump project list [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump project list --pretty
```

### brain-dump project find

Find a project by filesystem path

```bash
brain-dump project find --path <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--path`   | string  | Yes      | Filesystem path                       |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump project find --path /home/user/my-project
```

### brain-dump project create

Register a new project directory

```bash
brain-dump project create --name <value> --path <value> [--color <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--name`   | string  | Yes      | Project name                          |
| `--path`   | string  | Yes      | Filesystem path                       |
| `--color`  | string  | No       | Color hex (e.g. #3b82f6)              |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump project create --name "My App" --path /home/user/my-app
```

### brain-dump project delete

Delete a project and all associated data (dry-run by default)

```bash
brain-dump project delete --project <value> [--confirm] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | Yes      | Project ID                            |
| `--confirm` | boolean | No       | Confirm destructive action            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump project delete --project abc --confirm
```

## ticket

Create, list, get, update, delete tickets

### brain-dump ticket create

Create a new ticket

```bash
brain-dump ticket create --project <value> --title <value> [--description <value>] [--priority <low|medium|high>] [--epic <value>] [--tags <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project`     | string  | Yes      | Project ID                            |
| `--title`       | string  | Yes      | Ticket title                          |
| `--description` | string  | No       | Description                           |
| `--priority`    | enum    | No       | Priority level (low, medium, high)    |
| `--epic`        | string  | No       | Epic ID                               |
| `--tags`        | string  | No       | Comma-separated tags                  |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket create --project abc --title "Fix bug" --priority high
```

### brain-dump ticket list

List tickets with optional filters

```bash
brain-dump ticket list [--project <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket list --pretty
brain-dump ticket list --project abc --limit 10
```

### brain-dump ticket get

Get full ticket details

```bash
brain-dump ticket get --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket get --ticket abc --pretty
```

### brain-dump ticket update

Update ticket fields (title, description, status, priority, tags, epic)

```bash
brain-dump ticket update --ticket <value> [--title <value>] [--description <value>] [--status <backlog|ready|in_progress|ai_review|human_review|done>] [--priority <low|medium|high>] [--epic <value>] [--tags <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                                                                |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`      | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                                  |
| `--title`       | string  | No       | New title                                                                  |
| `--description` | string  | No       | New description                                                            |
| `--status`      | enum    | No       | Ticket status (backlog, ready, in_progress, ai_review, human_review, done) |
| `--priority`    | enum    | No       | Priority (low, medium, high)                                               |
| `--epic`        | string  | No       | Epic ID                                                                    |
| `--tags`        | string  | No       | Comma-separated tags                                                       |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                      |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket update --ticket abc --status done
brain-dump ticket update --ticket abc --title "New Title" --priority high
```

### brain-dump ticket update-status

Update ticket status (MCP naming alias)

```bash
brain-dump ticket update-status --ticket <value> --status <backlog|ready|in_progress|ai_review|human_review|done> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                                                                |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                                  |
| `--status` | enum    | Yes      | Ticket status (backlog, ready, in_progress, ai_review, human_review, done) |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                      |

### brain-dump ticket update-criterion

Update an acceptance criterion status

```bash
brain-dump ticket update-criterion --ticket <value> --criterion <value> --criterion-status <pending|passed|failed|skipped> [--note <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag                 | Type    | Required | Description                                         |
| -------------------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`           | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                           |
| `--criterion`        | string  | Yes      | Criterion ID                                        |
| `--criterion-status` | enum    | Yes      | Criterion status (pending, passed, failed, skipped) |
| `--note`             | string  | No       | Verification note                                   |
| `--pretty`           | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)               |

### brain-dump ticket update-attachment

Update attachment metadata

```bash
brain-dump ticket update-attachment --ticket <value> --attachment <value> [--attachment-type <value>] [--attachment-description <value>] [--attachment-priority <primary|supplementary>] [--linked-criteria <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag                       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| -------------------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`                 | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--attachment`             | string  | Yes      | Attachment ID or filename             |
| `--attachment-type`        | string  | No       | Attachment type                       |
| `--attachment-description` | string  | No       | Description                           |
| `--attachment-priority`    | enum    | No       | Priority (primary, supplementary)     |
| `--linked-criteria`        | string  | No       | Comma-separated criterion IDs         |
| `--pretty`                 | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump ticket list-by-epic

List tickets in an epic

```bash
brain-dump ticket list-by-epic --epic <value> [--project <value>] [--status <backlog|ready|in_progress|ai_review|human_review|done>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                                                   |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`    | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                                                                       |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                                                                    |
| `--status`  | enum    | No       | Filter by status (backlog, ready, in_progress, ai_review, human_review, done) |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                                                                   |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                         |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket list-by-epic --epic abc --pretty
```

### brain-dump ticket link-files

Link file paths to a ticket

```bash
brain-dump ticket link-files --ticket <value> --files <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--files`  | string  | Yes      | Comma-separated file paths            |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump ticket get-files

Find tickets linked to a file path

```bash
brain-dump ticket get-files --file <value> [--project <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--file`    | string  | Yes      | File path                             |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump ticket delete

Delete a ticket (dry-run by default)

```bash
brain-dump ticket delete --ticket <value> [--confirm] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--confirm` | boolean | No       | Confirm destructive action            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump ticket delete --ticket abc --confirm
```

## epic

Create, list, update, delete epics

### brain-dump epic create

Create a new epic

```bash
brain-dump epic create --project <value> --title <value> [--description <value>] [--color <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project`     | string  | Yes      | Project ID                            |
| `--title`       | string  | Yes      | Epic title                            |
| `--description` | string  | No       | Description                           |
| `--color`       | string  | No       | Color hex (e.g. #3b82f6)              |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic create --project abc --title "Auth System" --color "#3b82f6"
```

### brain-dump epic list

List epics for a project

```bash
brain-dump epic list --project <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | Yes      | Project ID                            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic list --project abc --pretty
```

### brain-dump epic update

Update epic title, description, or color

```bash
brain-dump epic update --epic <value> [--title <value>] [--description <value>] [--color <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`        | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                               |
| `--title`       | string  | No       | New title                             |
| `--description` | string  | No       | New description                       |
| `--color`       | string  | No       | New color hex                         |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic update --epic abc --title "New Title"
```

### brain-dump epic delete

Delete an epic (dry-run by default)

```bash
brain-dump epic delete --epic <value> [--confirm] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`    | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                               |
| `--confirm` | boolean | No       | Confirm destructive action            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic delete --epic abc --confirm
```

### brain-dump epic reconcile-learnings

Extract and store learnings from a completed ticket

```bash
brain-dump epic reconcile-learnings --ticket <value> --learnings-file <value> [--update-docs] [--pretty]
```

| Flag               | Type    | Required | Description                             |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`         | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                               |
| `--learnings-file` | string  | Yes      | Path to JSON file with learning objects |
| `--update-docs`    | boolean | No       | Apply suggested documentation updates   |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)   |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic reconcile-learnings --ticket abc --learnings-file ./learnings.json
```

### brain-dump epic get-learnings

Get accumulated learnings for an epic

```bash
brain-dump epic get-learnings --epic <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`   | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                               |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump epic get-learnings --epic abc --pretty
```

## workflow

Start work, complete work, start epic, launch Ralph

### brain-dump workflow start-work

Start work on a ticket (creates branch, updates status)

```bash
brain-dump workflow start-work --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump workflow start-work --ticket abc
```

### brain-dump workflow complete-work

Complete work on a ticket (moves to ai_review)

```bash
brain-dump workflow complete-work --ticket <value> [--summary <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--summary` | string  | No       | Work summary                          |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump workflow complete-work --ticket abc --summary "Implemented feature"
```

### brain-dump workflow start-epic

Start work on an epic (creates shared branch)

```bash
brain-dump workflow start-epic --epic <value> [--create-pr] [--pretty]
```

| Flag          | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`      | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                               |
| `--create-pr` | boolean | No       | Create draft PR                       |
| `--pretty`    | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump workflow start-epic --epic abc --create-pr
```

### brain-dump workflow launch-ticket

Launch Ralph for a single ticket in the chosen provider (parity with UI)

```bash
brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket <value> [--provider <claude-code|vscode|cursor|cursor-agent|copilot-cli|codex|opencode|pi>] [--terminal <value>] [--max-iterations <n>] [--sandbox] [--pretty]
```

| Flag               | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`         | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                                                                                     |
| `--provider`       | enum    | No       | AI backend to launch (default: project setting) (claude-code, vscode, cursor, cursor-agent, copilot-cli, codex, opencode, pi) |
| `--terminal`       | string  | No       | Preferred terminal emulator (e.g. ghostty, kitty, iterm2)                                                                     |
| `--max-iterations` | number  | No       | Override Ralph loop iteration cap                                                                                             |
| `--sandbox`        | boolean | No       | Run inside the Docker sandbox (claude-code only)                                                                              |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                                                                         |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket abc --provider claude-code
brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket abc --provider copilot-cli --terminal ghostty
brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket abc --provider pi --terminal ghostty
brain-dump workflow launch-ticket --ticket abc --provider claude-code --sandbox
```

### brain-dump workflow launch-epic

Launch Ralph for an entire epic in the chosen provider (parity with UI)

```bash
brain-dump workflow launch-epic --epic <value> [--provider <claude-code|vscode|cursor|cursor-agent|copilot-cli|codex|opencode|pi>] [--terminal <value>] [--max-iterations <n>] [--sandbox] [--pretty]
```

| Flag               | Type    | Required | Description                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`           | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                                                                                                                       |
| `--provider`       | enum    | No       | AI backend to launch (default: project setting) (claude-code, vscode, cursor, cursor-agent, copilot-cli, codex, opencode, pi) |
| `--terminal`       | string  | No       | Preferred terminal emulator (e.g. ghostty, kitty, iterm2)                                                                     |
| `--max-iterations` | number  | No       | Override Ralph loop iteration cap                                                                                             |
| `--sandbox`        | boolean | No       | Run inside the Docker sandbox (claude-code only)                                                                              |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                                                                         |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump workflow launch-epic --epic abc --provider claude-code
brain-dump workflow launch-epic --epic abc --provider opencode --max-iterations 20
brain-dump workflow launch-epic --epic abc --provider pi --max-iterations 20
```

---

## comment

Add and list ticket comments

### brain-dump comment add

Add a comment to a ticket

```bash
brain-dump comment add --ticket <value> --content <value> [--type <comment|work_summary|test_report|progress>] [--author <claude|ralph|user|opencode|cursor|vscode|ai>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                                        |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `--ticket`  | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                          |
| `--content` | string  | Yes      | Comment content                                                    |
| `--type`    | enum    | No       | Comment type (comment, work_summary, test_report, progress)        |
| `--author`  | enum    | No       | Comment author (claude, ralph, user, opencode, cursor, vscode, ai) |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                              |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump comment add --ticket abc --content "Fixed the bug"
```

### brain-dump comment list

List comments for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump comment list --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump comment list --ticket abc --pretty
```

---

## review

Submit findings, generate demos, manage reviews

### brain-dump review submit-finding

Submit a review finding for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump review submit-finding --ticket <value> --agent <code-reviewer|silent-failure-hunter|code-simplifier> --severity <critical|major|minor|suggestion> --category <value> --description <value> [--file <value>] [--line <n>] [--fix <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                                                          |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`      | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                            |
| `--agent`       | enum    | Yes      | Review agent (code-reviewer, silent-failure-hunter, code-simplifier) |
| `--severity`    | enum    | Yes      | Finding severity (critical, major, minor, suggestion)                |
| `--category`    | string  | Yes      | Finding category                                                     |
| `--description` | string  | Yes      | Finding description                                                  |
| `--file`        | string  | No       | File path                                                            |
| `--line`        | number  | No       | Line number                                                          |
| `--fix`         | string  | No       | Suggested fix                                                        |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                |

### brain-dump review mark-fixed

Mark a review finding as fixed, wont_fix, or duplicate

```bash
brain-dump review mark-fixed --finding <value> --status <fixed|wont_fix|duplicate> [--description <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                             |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--finding`     | string  | Yes      | Finding ID                              |
| `--status`      | enum    | Yes      | Fix status (fixed, wont_fix, duplicate) |
| `--description` | string  | No       | How it was fixed                        |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)   |

### brain-dump review check-complete

Check if all critical/major findings are resolved

```bash
brain-dump review check-complete --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump review generate-demo

Generate a demo script for human review

```bash
brain-dump review generate-demo --ticket <value> --steps-file <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag           | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`     | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--steps-file` | string  | Yes      | JSON file with demo steps             |
| `--pretty`     | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump review get-demo

Get the demo script for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump review get-demo --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump review submit-feedback

Submit demo feedback (human reviewer only)

```bash
brain-dump review submit-feedback --ticket <value> --passed --feedback <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag         | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ------------ | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`   | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--passed`   | boolean | Yes      | Whether demo passed                   |
| `--feedback` | string  | Yes      | Reviewer feedback                     |
| `--pretty`   | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump review update-demo-step

Update a demo step status during human review

```bash
brain-dump review update-demo-step --demo-script <value> --step-order <n> --step-status <pending|passed|failed|skipped> [--step-notes <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag            | Type    | Required | Description                                    |
| --------------- | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `--demo-script` | string  | Yes      | Demo script ID                                 |
| `--step-order`  | number  | Yes      | Step order number                              |
| `--step-status` | enum    | Yes      | Step status (pending, passed, failed, skipped) |
| `--step-notes`  | string  | No       | Reviewer notes                                 |
| `--pretty`      | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)          |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump review update-demo-step --demo-script abc --step-order 1 --step-status passed
```

### brain-dump review get-findings

Get review findings for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump review get-findings --ticket <value> [--status <open|fixed|wont_fix|duplicate>] [--severity <critical|major|minor|suggestion>] [--agent <code-reviewer|silent-failure-hunter|code-simplifier>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag         | Type    | Required | Description                                                             |
| ------------ | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`   | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                                                               |
| `--status`   | enum    | No       | Filter by status (open, fixed, wont_fix, duplicate)                     |
| `--severity` | enum    | No       | Filter by severity (critical, major, minor, suggestion)                 |
| `--agent`    | enum    | No       | Filter by agent (code-reviewer, silent-failure-hunter, code-simplifier) |
| `--pretty`   | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                   |

---

## session

Create, update, complete Ralph sessions

### brain-dump session create

Create a new Ralph session

```bash
brain-dump session create --ticket <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump session update

Update session state

```bash
brain-dump session update --session <value> --state <idle|analyzing|implementing|testing|committing|reviewing|done> [--message <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                                                         |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                                                          |
| `--state`   | enum    | Yes      | Session state (idle, analyzing, implementing, testing, committing, reviewing, done) |
| `--message` | string  | No       | State metadata message                                                              |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                               |

### brain-dump session complete

Complete a Ralph session

```bash
brain-dump session complete --session <value> --outcome <success|failure|timeout|cancelled> [--error <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                            |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                             |
| `--outcome` | enum    | Yes      | Session outcome (success, failure, timeout, cancelled) |
| `--error`   | string  | No       | Error message                                          |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                  |

### brain-dump session get

Get session details

```bash
brain-dump session get [--session <value>] [--ticket <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | No       | Session ID                            |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump session list

List sessions for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump session list --ticket <value> [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--limit`  | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump session update-state

Update session state (MCP naming alias)

```bash
brain-dump session update-state --session <value> --state <idle|analyzing|implementing|testing|committing|reviewing|done> [--message <value>] [--pretty]
```

**Aliases:** `update`

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                                                         |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                                                          |
| `--state`   | enum    | Yes      | Session state (idle, analyzing, implementing, testing, committing, reviewing, done) |
| `--message` | string  | No       | State metadata message                                                              |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                               |

### brain-dump session emit-event

Emit a real-time event for UI streaming

```bash
brain-dump session emit-event --session <value> --event-type <thinking|tool_start|tool_end|file_change|progress|state_change|error> [--event-data-file <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag                | Type    | Required | Description                                                                             |
| ------------------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--session`         | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                                                              |
| `--event-type`      | enum    | Yes      | Event type (thinking, tool_start, tool_end, file_change, progress, state_change, error) |
| `--event-data-file` | string  | No       | Path to JSON file with event data                                                       |
| `--pretty`          | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                                   |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump session emit-event --session abc --event-type progress
```

### brain-dump session get-events

Get events for a session

```bash
brain-dump session get-events --session <value> [--since <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--since`   | string  | No       | ISO timestamp filter                  |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump session get-events --session abc --limit 10 --pretty
```

### brain-dump session clear-events

Clear all events for a session

```bash
brain-dump session clear-events --session <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

---

## git

Link commits, PRs, sync ticket links

### brain-dump git link-commit

Link a git commit to a ticket

```bash
brain-dump git link-commit --ticket <value> --hash <value> [--message <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--hash`    | string  | Yes      | Git commit hash                       |
| `--message` | string  | No       | Commit message                        |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump git link-pr

Link a GitHub PR to a ticket

```bash
brain-dump git link-pr --ticket <value> --pr <n> [--url <value>] [--status <draft|open|merged|closed>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                             |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                               |
| `--pr`     | number  | Yes      | PR number                               |
| `--url`    | string  | No       | PR URL                                  |
| `--status` | enum    | No       | PR status (draft, open, merged, closed) |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)   |

### brain-dump git sync

Auto-discover and link commits/PRs to active ticket

```bash
brain-dump git sync [--project-path <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag             | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project-path` | string  | No       | Project path                          |
| `--pretty`       | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

---

## telemetry

Start, end, get, list telemetry sessions, record token usage

### brain-dump telemetry start

Start a telemetry session

```bash
brain-dump telemetry start [--ticket <value>] [--project <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project path                          |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump telemetry end

End a telemetry session

```bash
brain-dump telemetry end --session <value> [--outcome <success|failure|timeout|cancelled>] [--tokens <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                            |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------ |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                             |
| `--outcome` | enum    | No       | Session outcome (success, failure, timeout, cancelled) |
| `--tokens`  | number  | No       | Total token count                                      |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                  |

### brain-dump telemetry get

Get telemetry data for a session

```bash
brain-dump telemetry get [--session <value>] [--ticket <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | No       | Session ID                            |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump telemetry list

List telemetry sessions

```bash
brain-dump telemetry list [--ticket <value>] [--project <value>] [--since <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--since`   | string  | No       | ISO date filter                       |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump telemetry log-tool

Log a tool call event

```bash
brain-dump telemetry log-tool --session <value> --tool <value> --event <start|end> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--tool`    | string  | Yes      | Tool name                             |
| `--event`   | enum    | Yes      | Event type (start, end)               |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump telemetry log-prompt

Log a user prompt

```bash
brain-dump telemetry log-prompt --session <value> --prompt <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--prompt`  | string  | Yes      | Prompt text                           |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump telemetry log-context

Log ticket context loaded when AI started work

```bash
brain-dump telemetry log-context --session <value> [--has-description] [--has-criteria] [--criteria-count <n>] [--comment-count <n>] [--attachment-count <n>] [--image-count <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag                 | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| -------------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session`          | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--has-description`  | boolean | No       | Ticket had description                |
| `--has-criteria`     | boolean | No       | Ticket had acceptance criteria        |
| `--criteria-count`   | number  | No       | Number of criteria                    |
| `--comment-count`    | number  | No       | Number of comments                    |
| `--attachment-count` | number  | No       | Number of attachments                 |
| `--image-count`      | number  | No       | Number of images                      |
| `--pretty`           | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump telemetry log-context --session abc --has-description --has-criteria --criteria-count 3
```

### brain-dump telemetry record-usage

Record token usage with cost computation (auto-detects session)

```bash
brain-dump telemetry record-usage [--session <value>] [--ticket <value>] --model <value> --input <n> --output <n> [--cache-read <n>] [--cache-create <n>] [--source <jsonl-hook|otel|mcp-manual>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag             | Type    | Required | Description                                                            |
| ---------------- | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--session`      | string  | No       | Session ID                                                             |
| `--ticket`       | string  | No       | Ticket ID                                                              |
| `--model`        | string  | Yes      | Model name (e.g. claude-opus-4-6)                                      |
| `--input`        | number  | Yes      | Input token count                                                      |
| `--output`       | number  | Yes      | Output token count                                                     |
| `--cache-read`   | number  | No       | Cache read token count                                                 |
| `--cache-create` | number  | No       | Cache creation token count                                             |
| `--source`       | enum    | No       | Token data source (default: jsonl-hook) (jsonl-hook, otel, mcp-manual) |
| `--pretty`       | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                  |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump telemetry record-usage --model claude-opus-4-6 --input 1000 --output 500
brain-dump telemetry record-usage --session abc --model claude-opus-4-6 --input 1000 --output 500
```

### brain-dump telemetry recalculate-costs

Recalculate cost_usd for all token usage rows using current pricing models

```bash
brain-dump telemetry recalculate-costs [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump telemetry recalculate-costs --pretty
```

---

## files

Link files to tickets, find tickets by file

### brain-dump files link

Link file paths to a ticket

```bash
brain-dump files link --ticket <value> --files <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--files`  | string  | Yes      | Comma-separated file paths            |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump files get-tickets

Find tickets linked to a file

```bash
brain-dump files get-tickets --file <value> [--project <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--file`    | string  | Yes      | File path                             |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

## tasks

Save, get, clear Claude task lists

### brain-dump tasks save

Save Claude task list for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump tasks save [--ticket <value>] --tasks-file <value> [--snapshot] [--pretty]
```

| Flag           | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| -------------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`     | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--tasks-file` | string  | Yes      | JSON file with tasks                  |
| `--snapshot`   | boolean | No       | Create audit snapshot                 |
| `--pretty`     | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump tasks get

Get Claude tasks for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump tasks get [--ticket <value>] [--history] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--history` | boolean | No       | Include status history                |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump tasks clear

Clear all Claude tasks for a ticket

```bash
brain-dump tasks clear [--ticket <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump tasks snapshots

Get historical task snapshots

```bash
brain-dump tasks snapshots --ticket <value> [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--ticket` | string  | Yes      | Ticket ID                             |
| `--limit`  | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

## compliance

Conversation logging for compliance auditing

### brain-dump compliance start

Start a compliance conversation session

```bash
brain-dump compliance start [--project <value>] [--ticket <value>] [--user <value>] [--classification <public|internal|confidential|restricted>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag               | Type    | Required | Description                                                   |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--project`        | string  | No       | Project ID                                                    |
| `--ticket`         | string  | No       | Ticket ID                                                     |
| `--user`           | string  | No       | User identifier                                               |
| `--classification` | enum    | No       | Data sensitivity (public, internal, confidential, restricted) |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                         |

### brain-dump compliance log

Log a message to a conversation session

```bash
brain-dump compliance log --session <value> --role <user|assistant|system|tool> --content <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                                  |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                                   |
| `--role`    | enum    | Yes      | Message role (user, assistant, system, tool) |
| `--content` | string  | Yes      | Message content                              |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)        |

### brain-dump compliance end

End a conversation session

```bash
brain-dump compliance end --session <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--session` | string  | Yes      | Session ID                            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump compliance list

List conversation sessions

```bash
brain-dump compliance list [--project <value>] [--ticket <value>] [--since <value>] [--until <value>] [--limit <n>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--ticket`  | string  | No       | Ticket ID                             |
| `--since`   | string  | No       | Start date (ISO)                      |
| `--until`   | string  | No       | End date (ISO)                        |
| `--limit`   | number  | No       | Max results                           |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump compliance export

Export conversation logs for auditing

```bash
brain-dump compliance export --start <value> --end <value> [--session <value>] [--project <value>] [--verify] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--start`   | string  | Yes      | Start date (ISO)                      |
| `--end`     | string  | Yes      | End date (ISO)                        |
| `--session` | string  | No       | Session ID                            |
| `--project` | string  | No       | Project ID                            |
| `--verify`  | boolean | No       | Verify HMAC integrity                 |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump compliance archive

Archive old sessions (dry-run by default)

```bash
brain-dump compliance archive [--days <n>] [--confirm] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--days`    | number  | No       | Retention period in days              |
| `--confirm` | boolean | No       | Confirm destructive action            |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

---

```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |

| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

```

| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `--project`        | string  | Yes      | Project ID                                                                                     |
| `--working-method` | enum    | Yes      | Working method (auto, claude-code, vscode, opencode, cursor, cursor-agent, copilot-cli, codex) |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)                                                          |

## transfer

Export and import .braindump archives

### brain-dump transfer export-epic

Export an epic as a .braindump archive

```bash
brain-dump transfer export-epic --epic <value> [--output <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--epic`   | string  | Yes      | Epic ID                               |
| `--output` | string  | No       | Output file path                      |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump transfer export-project

Export a full project as a .braindump archive

```bash
brain-dump transfer export-project --project <value> [--output <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag        | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ----------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--project` | string  | Yes      | Project ID                            |
| `--output`  | string  | No       | Output file path                      |
| `--pretty`  | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump transfer import

Import a .braindump archive

```bash
brain-dump transfer import --file <value> [--target-project <value>] [--conflict <create-new|replace|merge>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag               | Type    | Required | Description                                      |
| ------------------ | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| `--file`           | string  | Yes      | Input .braindump file                            |
| `--target-project` | string  | No       | Target project ID                                |
| `--conflict`       | enum    | No       | Conflict resolution (create-new, replace, merge) |
| `--pretty`         | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON)            |

### brain-dump transfer preview

Preview a .braindump archive without importing

```bash
brain-dump transfer preview --file <value> [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--file`   | string  | Yes      | Input .braindump file                 |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

---

## admin

Backup, restore, check, doctor, health

### brain-dump admin backup

Create or list database backups

```bash
brain-dump admin backup [--list] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--list`   | boolean | No       | List available backups                |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump admin backup
brain-dump admin backup --list
```

### brain-dump admin restore

Restore database from a backup

```bash
brain-dump admin restore [--latest] [--file <value>] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--latest` | boolean | No       | Use most recent backup                |
| `--file`   | string  | No       | Specific backup file                  |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

### brain-dump admin check

Quick or full database integrity check

```bash
brain-dump admin check [--full] [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--full`   | boolean | No       | Run full health check                 |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump admin check
brain-dump admin check --full
```

### brain-dump admin doctor

Diagnose configuration issues

```bash
brain-dump admin doctor [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump admin doctor
```

### brain-dump admin health

Comprehensive database health report

```bash
brain-dump admin health [--pretty]
```

| Flag       | Type    | Required | Description                           |
| ---------- | ------- | -------- | ------------------------------------- |
| `--pretty` | boolean | No       | Human-readable output (default: JSON) |

**Examples:**

```bash
brain-dump admin health --pretty
```

---

## Environment

The CLI uses XDG-compliant paths:

| OS      | Database                                    | State (backups, logs)        |
| ------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- |
| Linux   | `~/.local/share/brain-dump/`                | `~/.local/state/brain-dump/` |
| macOS   | `~/Library/Application Support/brain-dump/` | Same                         |
| Windows | `%APPDATA%\brain-dump\`                     | Same                         |

The CLI shares the database with the Brain Dump web UI and MCP server.

## Exit Codes

| Code | Meaning                            |
| ---- | ---------------------------------- |
| 0    | Success                            |
| 1    | Error (validation, database, etc.) |
